Records.description: Three-loop massive sunset in $D=2$ with four distinct masses. Picard-Fuchs operator of order 6 computed. Geometry is a K3 surface in equal-mass case.
Three-loop four-mass banana. Maximal cut yields elliptically fibered K3 surface ($h^{1,1}=19$, $h^{2,0}=1$). 15 master integrals. Full DE left to future work.

Three-loop sunset in $D=2$ with 4 generic masses. Picard-Fuchs operator is order 6 for generic masses, associated to K3 surface. Order decreases to 5,4,4,3 for various mass specializations $[1^22],[22],[13],[4]$.

Records.authors: Duhr, Claude, Maggio, Sara, Porkert, Franziska, Semper, Cathrin, Stawinski, Sven F.
Records.description: Three-loop banana (sunrise-type) self-energy integral family with four distinct non-zero masses in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. The geometry is a family of K3 surfaces. A canonical (eps-factorized) differential equation system is constructed using twisted cohomology methods. The 15 master integrals include 4 tadpole subsectors, a corner integral, its mass derivatives, a second mass derivative, and 5 ISP integrals. The canonical DE matrix involves iterated integrals over rational functions, K3 periods and their derivatives, plus two genuinely new epsilon-functions that cannot be expressed in terms of periods alone.

Three-loop banana (watermelon) integral in $D=2$ with four distinct internal masses and external momentum $p$ (with $p^2$ normalized to 1). The maximal cut computes periods of a four-parameter family of K3 surfaces. The transcendental lattice is $T = H \oplus H \oplus A_2(-3)$ of discriminant $-3$. Via the exceptional isomorphism $O(2,4) \sim SU(2,2)$, the holomorphic period and mirror map are hermitian modular forms. The six-dimensional period vector is $(\Pi_2, \Pi_{14}, \Pi_{13}, \Pi_{12}, \Pi_{11}, \Pi_0)$, satisfying a bilinear Hodge-Riemann relation with the Gram matrix $\Sigma_4$.
Records.familyName: three-loop banana (four different masses, hermitian modular) / three-loop banana with four different masses (hermitian modular periods)
Records.method: parametric representation, Baikov representation, K3 periods, transcendental lattice analysis, orthogonal modular forms, mirror map, Hodge structure
Records.location: section 'Automorphic properties of three-loop banana integrals', subsection 'The case of four different masses' (bananas_2.tex)

Records.description: Three-loop banana (sunrise) diagram with four unequal internal masses $m_1, m_2, m_3, m_4$. External momentum squared set to $p^2=1$ (numerical). Five kinematic variables $(d, m_1^2, m_2^2, m_3^2, m_4^2)$. Reduced with rank-1 numerator. Demonstrates dramatic speedup (up to 9x) of FLINT/Symbolica over Fermat for many-scale problems.
3-loop banana integral with 4 generic masses. Holonomic rank $r=15=2^4-1$. The underlying geometry involves a K3 surface.

Records.authors: Pögel, Sebastian, Teschke, Toni, Wang, Xing, Weinzierl, Stefan
Records.description: Three-loop banana (sunrise/watermelon) two-point integral with four distinct internal masses $m_1, m_2, m_3, m_4$ in $D=2-2\varepsilon$ dimensions. The integral family has 4 propagators and 5 irreducible scalar products $(\sigma_5,\ldots,\sigma_9$, adapted to the loop-by-loop Baikov representation). There are 15 master integrals: 4 tadpoles (sectors with $\text{id} < 15$) and 11 in the top sector (sector 15). The maximal cut yields a two-dimensional Baikov representation with variables $\sigma_5, \sigma_6$. The underlying geometry is a K3 surface (Calabi-Yau 2-fold), realized as a double cover $y^2 - P(z_1,z_2) = 0$ in weighted projective space. The top-sector master integrals decompose via Hodge-theoretic filtrations $(F_{\text{comb}}, F_{\text{geom}}, W)$ into 6 integrals associated with the K3 cohomology and 5 integrals associated with isolated points having regulated logarithmic singularities. The eps-factorised differential equation is derived algorithmically using the method of Bree et al. (2025): first a basis $J$ is constructed with Laurent polynomial DE (orders $\varepsilon^{-2}$ to $\varepsilon^1$) compatible with the $F_{\text{comb}}$ filtration, then successive rotations $R_2^{(-2)}, R_2^{(-1)}, R_2^{(0)}$ eliminate non-eps-factorising terms. No prior knowledge of the K3 geometry is required. Numerical results for $K_5$ at order $\varepsilon^3$ and $\varepsilon^4$ are verified against AMFlow and sector decomposition to 9 digits. Phenomenological results are given for masses $(m_W, m_Z, m_H, m_t)$.
Records.location: Eq. (1)-(3) for the integral family definition; Eq. (6)-(8) for inverse propagators and Baikov variables; Eq. (13) for the starting basis I of 15 master integrals; Section 3 for basis J construction; Section 4 for basis K with eps-factorised DE; Section 5 for results

Records.description: Three-loop banana (sunrise-type) integral family with four unequal internal masses $m_1, m_2, m_3, m_4$ and off-shell external momentum $p$, evaluated in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. The family has 15 master integrals: 11 nontrivial top-level integrals and 4 trivial products of tadpole integrals. All 11 nontrivial master integrals are evaluated numerically via positivity constraints (semidefinite programming with SDPA-QD) at the kinematic point $p^2=2$, $m_1^2=2$, $m_2^2=3/2$, $m_3^2=4/3$, $m_4^2=1$, achieving $\sim 10^{-9}$ relative accuracy at $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^0)$. Epsilon expansion computed to $\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^2)$ via both direct positivity constraints and numerical differentiation. Reference results from DiffExp (series solutions of differential equations) used for validation. The three-loop banana with unequal masses is known to involve Calabi-Yau geometry.
Records.location: Eq. (3.1), Section 3, Fig. 2; master integrals Eqs. (3.2)-(3.4); numerical results Eqs. (3.14)-(3.16) at kinematic point Eq. (3.8)

Records.description: 3-loop unequal-mass banana reduction via top-sector ISP and loop-by-loop Baikov.

Records.authors: Bree, Iris, Gasparotto, Federico, Matijašić, Antonela, Mazloumi, Pouria, Melnichenko, Dmytro, Pögel, Sebastian, Teschke, Toni, Wang, Xing, Weinzierl, Stefan, Wu, Konglong, Xu, Xiaofeng
Records.description: Three-loop banana (sunrise) with four distinct internal masses. 4 propagators + 5 ISPs. 15 master integrals (11 in top sector + 4 tadpoles). K3 surface geometry with four moduli. Epsilon-factorization requires Picard-Fuchs operators and Frobenius period solutions. Used as the Calabi-Yau example for the algorithm.

Unequal-mass three-loop banana graph family with 4 massive propagators carrying distinct masses $m_1$-$m_4$ in $d=2-2\varepsilon$. 15 master integrals with 11 coupled in the top sector. Associated with a Calabi-Yau 2-fold. Cross-checked against pySecDec.

Three-loop banana (sunrise) diagram with four unequal internal masses m1, m2, m3, m4. External momentum squared set to p^2=1 (numerical). Five kinematic variables (d, m1^2, m2^2, m3^2, m4^2). Reduced with rank-1 numerator. Demonstrates dramatic speedup (up to 9x) of FLINT/Symbolica over Fermat for many-scale problems.

Records.authors: Fevola, C., Mizera, S., Telen, S.
Records.description: Landau singularities of the 4-banana with generic internal, zero external masses, computed via principal Landau determinants.
